claims.text: POLARIS is a modular orchestration framework for back-office automation that treats the process as planning over typed agents, using a Chain-of-Action planner for type-checked DAGs, a rubric-based selector for policy-aware selection, and guarded execution with validators and compiled policy checks.

The paper presents a comprehensive survey methodology that systematically examines trustworthy agentic AI through two core dimensions (Safety and Robustness, Privacy and System Security), identifying risks across agent workflows, and consolidating evaluation into a unified metrics-and-benchmarks framework.
The paper is a literature review and conceptual analysis, not a technical study with a specific method.
Research paper: Securing the Agent: Vendor-Neutral, Multitenant Enterprise Retrieval and Tool Use
Proposes using MVCC principles to manage concurrency in lakehouses for AI agents, with data and compute isolation through a unified API to simplify governance challenges.
claims.best_result: Not applicable - this is a position paper without empirical results

claims.text: The paper presents a socio-technical analysis of agentic AI using the MAD-BAD-SAD construct as an analytical lens, examining how architectural choices in perception, cognition, planning, execution, and memory introduce dependencies related to data governance, accountability, transparency, safety, and sustainability.
Research paper: Caging the Agents: A Zero Trust Security Architecture for Autonomous AI in Healthcare
Research paper: PaperOrchestra: A Multi-Agent Framework for Automated AI Research Paper Writing

Analyzed 1,178 safety and reliability papers from 9,439 generative AI papers written by 5 AI companies and 6 universities using OpenAI's o3-mini for classification and regex keyword searches for high-risk domains
claims.best_result: AI governance research is highly concentrated within a handful of uniquely resourced and integrated AI tech companies

claims.text: The paper describes a conceptual framework for integrating generative AI into information access systems, focusing on two paradigms: direct information generation and information synthesis.
Research paper: Claw AI Lab: An Autonomous Multi-Agent Research Team

The authors organized a hackathon to explore the use of generative AI for research and education in materials science, focusing on participants' ability to critically evaluate and use AI-generated content for software trials and development.
This paper is a chapter discussing research challenges and opportunities in agentic AI reliability, focusing on topics like cascading failures, dynamic environments, and testing methodologies.

Paper2Agent transforms static research papers into interactive AI agents by extracting the associated codebase, setting up a reproducible environment, wrapping core analytical features as MCP (Model Context Protocol) tools, and deploying a remote server that an AI agent can connect to for natural language interaction with the paper's methods.
Research paper: Right-to-Act: A Pre-Execution Non-Compensatory Decision Protocol for AI Systems
The paper introduces a benchmark called Sola-Visibility-ISPM to evaluate agentic AI systems on Identity Security Posture Management tasks, defining nine operational dimensions for assessment.
Research paper: Beyond principlism: Practical strategies for ethical AI use in research practices
Research paper: SBOMs into Agentic AIBOMs: Schema Extensions, Agentic Orchestration, and Reproducibility Evaluation
Research paper: Persistent AI Agents in Academic Research: A Single-Investigator Implementation Case Study

The paper introduces programmable lakehouse abstractions for modeling the data lifecycle, operationalizes a prototype for pipeline repair using a ReAct loop, and addresses safety objections for high-stakes automation.
Proposes agentic architectures that reconcile traditional autonomous agent concepts with modern generative models, focusing on reliability through componentisation, interfaces, and supervision.
Research paper: AEMA: Verifiable Evaluation Framework for Trustworthy and Controlled Agentic LLM Systems

The study employs a granular decomposition of NIST CSF 2.0 functions into specific tasks, linking essential AI agent properties (autonomy, adaptive learning, real-time responsiveness) to subcategory security requirements, and outlines graduated levels of autonomy (assisted, augmented, fully autonomous).

A head-to-head comparison of two agentic AI systems (Claude Code and Codex) in performing a complete scientific analysis pipeline on simulated gravitational wave data, from signal recovery to manuscript generation.
datasets: Simulated binary black hole signal injections for the Einstein Telescope
claims.best_result: Claude Code completed the pipeline significantly faster (~3.4 min vs ~16 min) while achieving scientific convergence with Codex.

claims.text: End-to-end analysis of AI across the complete research lifecycle, organized into four epistemological phases: Creation, Writing, Validation, and Dissemination, studying developments through April 2026.

The authors built a high-throughput automated executor to implement and evaluate LLM-generated research ideas in parallel, using the execution results as feedback to guide an evolutionary search and reinforcement learning process for the ideator model.
claims.best_result: Execution-guided evolutionary search found a post-training recipe outperforming the GRPO baseline by 21.4 percentage points on math reasoning.

claims.text: The paper applies methods from quantum physics and machine learning to astrophysical data.
This paper presents a survey and framework for AutoResearch, analyzing the spectrum of AI-powered scientific workflow automation from human-steered 'Vibe Research' to fully autonomous AI-led systems.
Research paper: AI-Researcher: Autonomous Scientific Innovation
Research paper: The AI Scientist: Towards Fully Automated Open-Ended Scientific Discovery
Research paper: Robin: A multi-agent system for automating scientific discovery
The paper describes a software tool that generates academic articles by analyzing Python code and datasets, without using advanced language models.
A declarative stack that encodes experiments as configurations, which are compiled to device-level APIs for AI-driven scientific discovery.

The AI Scientist-v2 uses a progressive agentic tree-search methodology managed by an experiment manager agent, eliminates human-authored code templates, and integrates a Vision-Language Model feedback loop for figure refinement.
claims.best_result: One manuscript exceeded the average human acceptance threshold at ICLR workshop peer review

claims.text: Research paper: Honest Lying: Understanding Memory Confabulation in Reflexive Agents
Conceptual analysis of XAI challenges for BLV users, identifying the modality gap and proposing accessible XAI approaches
Research paper: Mitigating Perceptual Judgment Bias in Multimodal LLM-as-a-Judge via Perceptual Perturbation and Rew
Research paper: ProtoAda: Prototype-Guided Adaptive Adapter Expansion and Geometric Consolidation for Multimodal Con
Research paper: AdaCodec: A Predictive Visual Code for Video MLLMs

ClinEnv is an interactive benchmark that evaluates LLMs as attending physicians through Longitudinal Inpatient Simulation, where models must query four specialized agents at each decision stage before committing to medications, procedures, and diagnoses.
claims.best_result: 0.31 decision F1 for the strongest model

claims.text: Research paper: IntraShuffler: A Privacy Preserving Framework for Heterogeneous DP Federated Learning
Research paper: Permissive Safety Through Trusted Inference: Verifiable Belief-Space Neural Safety Filters for Assur
Research paper: From Layers to Submodules: Rethinking Granularity in Replacement-Based LLM Compression

MDA (Mixture-Density Representation) predicts multiple depth hypotheses and their probabilities per pixel to address depth ambiguity at boundaries, extending naturally to transparent objects and sky regions.
Research paper: SimSD: Simple Speculative Decoding in Diffusion Language Models
They define traits as directions in text embedding space, train a linear model on labeled skill diffs, and score skill edits by projecting embedding diffs onto trait vectors.
datasets: 68 labeled skill diff pairs for the trait of propensity to seek sensitive data
claims.best_result: 91.2% sign classification accuracy on 68 labeled skill diff pairs

claims.text: Research paper: SafeSteer: Localized On-Policy Distillation for Efficient Safety Alignment
Research paper: Auditing Asset-Specific Preferences in Financial Large Language Models: Evidence from Bitcoin Repres
Research paper: Why Not Hyperparameter-Friendly Optimisation? A Monotonic Adaptive Norm Rescaling Approach For Long-

Introduces Moment-Video, a benchmark with 1,000 human-verified video-QA pairs across 7 domains and 25 subcategories, covering four task types: Temporal Occurrence, Temporal Counting, Action Description, and Temporal Reasoning.

DrPO samples candidates from the current generator, ranks them with a target reward, and uses high- and low-scoring samples to synthesize a feature-space update direction consisting of a non-parametric dipole preference field plus reference drift.
claims.best_result: Reduces HPSv3 training computation by 3.51× under matched effective-batch setting

claims.text: Optimal Mixture Transport (OMT) reformulates the transport problem as a strictly biconvex optimization over mixture models, decoupling computational complexity from sample size.

Survey and comparative analysis of generative models for inverse materials design, examining variational autoencoders, normalizing flows, autoregressive formulations, and diffusion models with multimodal learning approaches.
Research paper: Bridging the Last Mile of Time Series Forecasting with LLM Agents
Three-tiered monitoring approach (within-run, cross-run, structural) using variance-based characterization with FMEA-inspired severity classification to prioritize findings
datasets: synthetic testbed with 220 runs across 120 document bundles
claims.best_result: Structural monitor identifies integration gap with perfect consistency (CV = 0.00)

claims.text: Research paper: Expressivity of congruence-based architectures for DNNs on positive-definite matrices
Research paper: RASER: Recoverability-Aware Selective Escalation Router for Multi-Hop Question Answering
Research paper: Iteris: Agentic Research Loops for Computational Mathematics
Implementation of Speculative Tool Privacy Contracts as a runtime abstraction to handle observation before commitment as a first-class effect, distinct from state mutation.
Uses Physics-Informed Neural Network residuals as cellwise indicators for adaptive mesh refinement in finite-difference PDE solvers
claims.best_result: PINN-threshold refinement achieves relative L² error 0.021067 with 60 degrees of freedom, compared to 0.022617 for uniform refinement with 192 degrees of freedom

claims.text: Research paper: MCP-Persona: Benchmarking LLM Agents on Real-World Personal Applications via Environment Simulation
Agent Laboratory is a framework that uses LLM agents to automate the research process in three stages: literature review, experimentation, and report writing, based on a human-provided research idea.
claims.best_result: Agent Laboratory driven by o1-preview generates the best research outcomes and achieves state-of-the-art performance in machine learning code.

claims.text: Research paper: MLE-bench: Evaluating Machine Learning Agents on Machine Learning Engineering
Research paper: AIBuildAI-2: Scalable Agentic AI Research and Engineering
Research paper: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning
Research paper: Self-Refine: Iterative Refinement with Self-Feedback
Research paper: Voyager: An Open-Ended Embodied Agent with Large Language Models

AgentBench is a multi-dimensional benchmark designed to evaluate LLM-as-Agent across a spectrum of different environments, encompassing eight distinct environments categorized into three types of groundings: Code, Game, and Web.
Research paper: SWE-agent: Agent-Computer Interfaces Enable Automated Software Engineering
